about: Appsilon is a pioneering technology company that produces lab-grown diamonds using CVD (Chemical Vapor Deposition) technology—a capability held by only a few organizations worldwide. As the first and only producer in Türkiye and the largest in Europe, Appsilon develops high-performance materials that possess the exact physical, chemical, and optical properties of natural diamonds, but with significantly larger dimensions and precisely controlled compositions. These materials play a critical role in defense, aerospace, electronics, and optical systems. Appsilon operates with full vertical integration and zero external dependency. By designing and manufacturing its own diamond growth reactors, developing proprietary growth recipes, and utilizing in-house cutting and polishing equipment, the company manages the entire production cycle under one roof. Application Areas Thermal Management: Leveraging diamond’s status as the material with the highest known thermal conductivity, Appsilon provides cooling solutions for high-power RF components (GaN, SiC) and laser systems. Optical Systems: The company develops diamond components that offer superior durability and transmittance for Infrared (IR) and high-energy laser windows. Due to its high thermal conductivity, diamond uniquely serves a dual function in these systems. Quantum Technologies: Appsilon produces specialized, high-purity diamond-based materials containing Nitrogen-Vacancy (NV) centers for quantum sensors and processors. Semiconductors and Electronics: It provides diamond-based substrates for next-generation power electronics and high-frequency devices. Strategic Vision "By localizing strategically critical diamond technologies, Appsilon aims to bolster the technological independence of Türkiye’s defense and aerospace ecosystem." Strategic R&D: Through operations based in Istanbul and Delft, Appsilon has established strategic partnerships with global universities and defense industry giants. Sustainability: The company adopts a high-tech production model that is both ethical and characterized by a low environmental footprint.

about: ASPİLSAN Enerji started producing radio batteries in 1985 and began manufacturing aviation and helicopter batteries with Ni-Cd chemistry in 1993. The main activities and solutions of ASPİLSAN Enerji are as follows: • Production of INR18650 Lithium-Ion Rechargeable Cylindrical Cells • Sales of Batteries with Different Chemistries • Radio and Weapon Battery Systems • Telecommunication Batteries • Medical Battery Systems • Mini UPS and Energy Storage Systems • Aviation Battery Systems and Cells (Lithium-Ion and Nickel-Cadmium) • Marine Battery Systems • Rail Battery Systems • Robotic and Autonomous Battery Systems • Engineering and Testing Services • Electronic Card Assembly In addition to being the first and only company in Türkiye producing aviation/helicopter battery systems with Ni-Cd chemistry, ASPİLSAN Enerji is also the largest battery manufacturer in the country. ASPİLSAN Enerji provides all its solutions and services under the following quality management certifications: • IRIS ISO 22163: International Railway Industry Standard • AQAP 2110: NATO Quality Assurance Requirements for Design, Development and Production • ISO 9001:2015: Quality Management Systems Standard of the International Organization for Standardization • ISO 14001: Environmental Management Systems • ISO 45001: Occupational Health and Safety Management Systems ASPİLSAN Enerji started mass production of its Lithium-Ion Rechargeable Cylindrical Cell ASPİLSAN INR18650A28 in June 2022 and became the first company in Europe to mass-produce lithium-ion 18650 cells. The design, development, and production of the cell are entirely domestic and national. All intellectual and industrial property rights of the cell belong to ASPİLSAN Enerji. The chemistry and composition of the ASPİLSAN INR18650A28 is nickel-rich lithium nickel manganese cobalt oxide. Its size is 18650, capacity is 2800 mAh, and nominal voltage is 3.65 V. ASPİLSAN Enerji’s lithium-ion 18650 cell has proven to offer higher discharge rates and the ability to operate at lower temperatures compared to similar-capacity cells of well-known certified brands. The lithium-ion cell production facility has an annual production capacity of 220 MWh. The production line is capable of manufacturing 21,000,000 cells per year. The facility infrastructure and production line are designed not only to produce cells with NMC chemistry but also to manufacture 18650 and 21700 lithium-ion cells with NCA, LFP, LCO, LMO, and LTO chemistries. The safety and performance certifications obtained for the ASPİLSAN INR18650A28 cell are as follows: • UN DOT 38.3: Safety Test Standard for International Air Transportation • IEC 62133-2: Secondary cells and batteries containing alkaline or other non-acid electrolytes – Safety requirements for portable sealed secondary cells and batteries made from them for portable applications • IEC 61960-3: Secondary cells and batteries containing alkaline or other non-acid electrolytes – Secondary sealed cells and portable batteries – Part 3: Prismatic and cylindrical lithium secondary cells and batteries made from them Lithium-ion cells produced by ASPİLSAN Enerji can be used in radio systems, jammers, robotic and weapon systems, power tools, medical batteries, hybrid vehicles, smart textile batteries, e-bikes and scooters, forklifts, UPS systems, and energy storage systems. In addition to its current product portfolio, ASPİLSAN Enerji designs and develops project-based products tailored to customer needs.

about: Since 1979 Atlantic Fluid Tech has been manufacturing in Modena, Italy, hydraulic valves and components with high performance and quality standards. Under the name of CHS , the company was founded in 1979 with the aim of manufacturing aluminium, steel and cast iron blocks, specifically meant for the hydraulic market, which was an emerging industry at the time. In the ‘90s it specialized in production, assembly and testing of finished hydraulic valves and integrated circuits, designed by third parties. It soon proved to be one of the most qualified supplier in the field. In 2008 Atlantic Fluid Tech was established with the mission of designing, developing and selling its own hydraulic valves and integrated blocks. It’s 2011 when the two companies merged keeping the name of Atlantic Fluid Tech and a new industrial entity was formed to combine modern design with extensive production expertise. Its main strength turned out to be ad-hoc personalised engineering under specific customer’s request. Today the company is also offering a complete line of standard hydraulic products , especially diverter valves, pressure reducing valves, flow control valves, relief valves, hydraulic metering valves, overcentre valves and cartridges, proportional flow divider valves and hydraulic flow divider combiners, modular valves, check valves and several versions of solenoid cartridge valve, high pressure cartridge valve and pressure reducing valve. We manufacture in 6 production plants for an overall covered surface of 15,400 sqm : the two new AFT units which  have been equipped with the state-of-the-art production technologies and the four sister companies’ ones, specialized in heat treatment and thermal deburring, turning, production of cartridges and manifolds. We produce in Emilia , a land of excellence in mechanics and with a long tradition in the hydraulic field. That’s why we can count on a local highly-qualified supplier chain. Investments and innovation have always been, right from the beginning, essential factors in our corporate mission and this makes us one of the most dynamic manufacturers in the field. We strongly believe in IT and Industry 4.0 principles applying advanced technology to company processes and manufcturing, as they can create new business models and improve general working conditions. Thanks to our technical expertise and reliability, we count among our partners the leading world companies in several applications , like agriculture and service machines, earth moving machines, drilling and lifting equipment, industrial vehicles, industrial automation. To stand by our customers and guarantee the best technical and logistic support, we have opened 6 branches in Japan, China, South Korea, France, United Kingdom, Holland and established a widespread distribution network in the United States, Australia and many other countries. That’s why whent it comes to technical expertise, reliability and innovation, there’s no better partner in hydraulics than Atlantic Fluid Tech. In the picture below, Christian Storci, Managing Director and Michele Storci, Financial Director.

about: BFK Marin Ventilation was established on 23.08.2024 and operates primarily in the marine sector, as well as in industrial fields, providing manufacturing, sales, installation, maintenance, and repair services for ventilation ducts and equipment. Our company specializes in insulated and non-insulated air ducts, fittings, and fire dampers manufactured from aluminum and galvanized materials. We are proud to be the only domestic company capable of producing these products with MED certification. In this context, we have developed the “NanogenAir Project,” an innovative air sterilization system consisting of an ultraviolet (UV) light source, a stainless steel protective housing, and an advanced electronic control unit. The interaction of UV rays with the stainless steel surface generates a next-generation hygienic plasma, which contains inactivation agents and negative ions. These are distributed throughout the environment via air circulation, effectively neutralizing harmful microorganisms such as bacteria, viruses, and mold/fungi. Additionally, the plasma ion technology facilitates the precipitation of airborne particles, providing a cleaner and healthier breathing environment. Our project offers significant advantages, particularly for defence industry applications. By utilizing aluminum-based materials and laser welding technology, we provide lighter, more durable, and corrosion-resistant ventilation systems. When integrated with antibacterial duct systems and UV-based air purification devices, our solution enhances indoor air quality in military vessels. This contributes to protecting crew health, improving operational efficiency, and increasing system reliability.

Today, with a team of 100 experts, BOSAN provides high-quality services in compliance with military standards in the following areas: * Aluminum conversion coating (Alodine) * Anodizing (Anodic oxidation) coatings * Zinc, Zinc-Nickel, Phosphate, and Passivation coatings * Precious metal coatings (Copper, Nickel, Tin, Silver, Gold) * Electroless nickel plating * Wet paint, camouflage paint, electrostatic powder coating, Cerakote coating * Sandblasting and surface preparation processes BOSAN holds critical quality and environmental management certifications such as ISO 45001, ISO 14001, and AS 9100 Rev D. It has achieved a B rating from EYDEP and has adopted lean manufacturing and efficiency practices through the “learn-transform” approach after completing Model Factory training. The company maintains long-term collaborations with leading industry organizations such as ASELSAN, ROKETSAN, BAYKAR, MKEK, HAVELSAN, and TÜBİTAK, and has been serving as an approved supplier to ROKETSAN for over 15 years.

Boasting over 100 related intellectual property rights, the company is headquartered in Wuxi (Jiangsu Province) – home to the Wuxi Inertial Sensing Engineering Technology Research Center – with subsidiaries in Beijing, Chengdu, Hangzhou, and other cities. Guided by the vision of leading global inertial sensor market development, BWSENSING focuses on inertial sensor modules and upstream components. Its independently developed intelligent sensor portfolio includes Beidou-integrated navigation systems, inertial measurement units (IMUs), attitude and heading reference systems (AHRS), fiber optic gyroscopes, electronic compasses, and tilt sensors, fully addressing diverse needs for inertial attitude measurement and high-precision navigation. about: The establishment of our company dates back to 1993. Our company, which was founded to realize various engineering projects, began to implement various structures, especially boats, using only composite materials through a radical change in 2010. Due to the founding family’s passion for sailing and boats, the production of racing sailboats became the main concentration of our company in the initial stage. However, not only the main components of the boat, such as the hull and deck, but also the essential parts of a racing boat, such as carbon rudder, mast, and deck fittings, were chosen to be produced in-house to increase the company’s experience with different materials and production methods instead of sourcing them externally. The boats we produce as CSC Composites have been exported to many countries around the world. These boats have made unparalleled contributions to our country’s success in this sector by ranking in the top 3 in the world’s most challenging and prestigious races, despite tough competitors and conditions. During this process, high-tech composite productions have been carried out for various sectors, primarily the marine industry. Almost all of these products have been exported and started to be used in different parts of the world. For more details about this period, you can visit our boats page. In 2017, we brought to life all the chassis and body parts of Turkey’s first electric concept car using carbon fiber. In the following year, we started the mass production of French Phantom catamarans, known as hydrofoils, which were the most innovative platform of the time and flew above the water thanks to their wings. Within two years, we produced a total of 60 boats and wings and exported all of them. In 2019, we took a step into the defence and aviation industry by developing the composite part of Albatros, the largest propeller-driven drone platform implemented in our country, in a very short time in collaboration with Altınay, the project owner. In the same years, we started to participate in the Superyacht industry, where our country has an important share, with our own design and engineering using carbon fiber equipment. With our increasing experience, capabilities, investments, and expanding product portfolio, we continue to carry out productions for many companies both domestically and internationally In 2022, in line with our expanding capacity, we started the construction of our new factory in the Kınık district of İzmir province. We have started the construction of our factory, which is designed entirely for composite production and will have an area of approximately 5,500 m² and a closed area of 2,000 m², and we plan to start operations in our new facility before the middle of 2023.

about: General Directorate of State Airports Authority​​ (DHMI) is responsible for the management of Turkish airports and the regulation and control of Turkish airspace. With its facilities and equipment that provide the infrastructure for the Turkish civil aviation industry, DHMI has provided airport management and air traffic services under different names and status since 1933. With the introduction of Law Decree numbered 233 and the Principle Statute in 1984, DHMI continued its activities as a state-owned enterprise. As a legal entity that has autonomy over its activities, DHMI is a state-owned enterprise (SOE) that is associated with The Ministry of Transport and Infrastructure. Its liability is limited to its capital. The status of the DHMI has been retained by the latest presidential decree. The DHMI`s purpose and subject of activities are defined in its principle statute as follows: Air transport required by civil aviation activities, management of airports, providing ground services at airports and air traffic control services, establishment and operation of air navigation systems and facilities and other related facilities and systems, and to maintain them at the level of modern aviation. DHMI is committed to perform its undertaken tasks in compliance with the international civil aviation rules and standards. In line with this aim, DHMI is a member of International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO), which was founded according to Civil Aviation Agreement that came into force in order to ensure safety of life and property in international aviation and to maintain regular economic working and progress. Furthermore, DHMI is also a member of relevant international organizations such as the EUROCONTROL and the Airports Council International (ACI). As part of air navigation and airport management services provided by the DHMI, traffic of airplanes and passengers has significantly increased in recent years. Especially, there has been significant progress in terms of international flights and passenger traffic at the international airports. Today, Istanbul Airport and Antalya Airport are among the leading airports of Europe owing to the growth in international traffic. ​

about: DURATEK has been designing, producing and marketing its epoxy, polyurethane and acrylic based system with its almost 50 years of experience in polymer chemistry. DURATEK is 100% Turkish company and its headquarter and production facility are based in Gebze Organised Industrial Zone (GOSB). Construction, marine, composite and defence industries are the main industries for DURATEK. DURATEK’s product pallet for defence industry can be summarized as following. 1. Composite Products: DURATEK has epoxy based lamination resins suitable for different application techniques such as hand lamination, vacuum bagging, vacuum infusion, RTM, L-RTM, filament winding, centrifugal casting and pultrusion. DURATEK also provides epoxy and polyurethane based structural adhesives for composites industry. These products can be used in different application fields such as marine vessels, wind turbine blades, defence industry products such as rocket bodies. Almost every structural product of DURATEK is approved by DNV-GL, Lloyd’s Register or Rina. 2. PRC Floor Coating for the Floors of Naval Ships: The floors of the ships of the naval ships belonging to the Turkish Navy and the Coast Guard have been coated with DURATEK’s PRC system. This system consists of several layers of epoxy and polyurethane based coats. It’s fire retardancy, low smoke toxicity and low smoke density properties are approved according to IMO Res. MSC.61 (67) - (FTP Code) Annex 1 Part 2 and Part 5, IMO MSC / Circ. 1120, IMO Res. MSC.307(88) - (2010 FTP Code) Section 8 standards by Lloyd’s Register. DURATEK’s PRC system also has a NATO number: T9967. 3. Nonskid Helipad Coating DURATEK’s helipad coating was previously applied on Turkish Naval Ship Muavenet and on 167 meter Dubai Yacht, which was constructed as Platinum Project. New version of it is developed together with Gölcük Naval Shipyard. This version was applied on TCG FATIH Naval Ship with the help of HELIPLAT. Duratek’s nonskid helipad coating completed its trial period successfully and the Strategic Collaboration Agreement for the product had been signed with the Directorate General of Naval Shipyards. It was applied on first LHD of Navy, TCG ANADOLU, TCG DERYA (DIMDEG), TCG İSTANBUL, Naval Ships of the Turkish Coast Guard (TCSG YASAM, TCSG UMUT, TCSG DOST, TCSG GUVEN) and Qatar Offshore Patrol Vessels (OPV). Duratek’s nonskid helipad coating’s physical, mechanical (static abrasion resistance, impact resistance, corrosion resistance, etc.) and chemical properties tested according to MIL PRF-24667C standards by national and international institutions. This product has fire retardancy, low smoke toxicity and low smoke density properties as well. These properties are certified according to IMO MSC 61(67) Part 2 and 5. 4. Epoxy and Polyurethane Based Paints for Steel Surfaces DURATEK’s epoxy and polyurethane based anticorrosive and UV resistant paints can be used on floors with light or heavy traffic, ballast, fuel, fresh or waste water tanks and other steel surfaces. DURATEK has a membership to or is a board member of the following NGO’s: Turkish Composite Manufacturer’s Association, SA-HA Istanbul, ENSİA, ARUS, Turkish Corrosion Association, TUREB, BOSAD, TKSD, GOSBSAD, İMMİB, Kocaeli Chamber of Industry, and Gebze Chamber of Commerce.

about: EarFit is a technology company specializing in high-performance acoustic solutions, personalized communication systems, and advanced material engineering for the defence and aerospace industries. The company’s core expertise lies in developing high-durability Titanium-bodied acoustic communication systems that are 100% compatible with the user’s anatomical structure, utilizing metal additive manufacturing technologies. Through these advanced manufacturing and material capabilities, EarFit provides systems that ensure maximum isolation and crystal-clear communication transmission—surpassing the limitations of standard equipment—particularly in aviation platforms and operational environments where noise levels are critical. EarFit is not merely a hardware developer, but an engineering center conducting R&D in acoustic stealth and health data tracking. The flagship of the product line, the Titanium Communication Platform, offers full resistance to extreme pressure and temperature conditions through the use of aerospace-grade Titanium. These systems will equipped with integrated health sensors to ensure the operational continuity of mission-critical personnel, enabling real-time monitoring of vital signs during active duty. EarFit completely eliminates the "environmental noise and physical incompatibility" issues—the weakest links in the communication chain between aircraft and command-and-control centers—through personalized design and advanced acoustic technologies. EarFit’s defence vision extends beyond passive protection systems to encompass active acoustic management strategies. Specifically, the acoustic signature reduction technologies and metamaterial-based damping solutions developed for underwater platforms minimize the risk of detection by lowering the acoustic footprint. Integrating advanced manufacturing techniques, sensor fusion, and acoustic science under one roof, EarFit delivers innovative solutions for the warfare technologies of the future, ranging from pilot helmet integrations to underwater acoustic invisibility projects.

about: ECEMTAG Control Technologies Inc. was established in February 2018 with the motivation to localize engine and transmission control technologies. Our company provides electronic hardware and software development, project execution, and consultancy services for the control, monitoring, and protection of diesel and gasoline engines, as well as automatic transmission systems used in automotive, marine, railway, construction machinery, and military platforms. In addition, we offer solutions for electronic control units for next-generation electric and autonomous vehicles. Within 8 years, ECEMTAG has built a team of more than 40 specialized engineers and continues to develop Engine Control Units (ECU) and Transmission Control Units (TCU) targeting both domestic and international heavy-duty powertrain markets. All electronic and mechanical hardware design, real-time embedded software development, and control algorithm design activities are carried out entirely in-house. The company also produces functional prototypes that are actively tested in the field. ECEMTAG has the capability to design, develop, manufacture, test, and validate engine and transmission control units in the power range of 20 to 20,000 kW. Currently, within the scope of the strategic programs of the Presidency of Defence Industries, ECEMTAG is successfully executing ECU and TCU design and development activities for the Turkish Main Battle Tank and Next-Generation Light Armored Vehicle projects. The solutions developed in these projects control 8- and 12-cylinder V-type engines and 6-speed fully automatic transmissions, delivering 1,000 HP and 1,500 HP power outputs respectively for tracked military land vehicles. In recent years, we have also been successfully controlling military vehicle powertrains in the 50–750 HP range with our control unit solutions developed for 4- and 6-cylinder inline diesel engines. Verification and testing processes constitute one of our highest priorities during development. In this context, thanks to our in-house Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) infrastructure, we are able to test and validate our software and hardware solutions in a highly accurate virtual environment before integration into real vehicles. This approach significantly reduces development time and minimizes field risks. ECEMTAG provides cost-effective, reliable, and open-platform development tools suitable for projects of all scales. By utilizing the latest technologies such as scalable semiconductors and model-based design tools, we significantly accelerate the development process. With our experience in automotive software and hardware development, we offer development and consultancy services compliant with ASPICE, Functional Safety (ISO 26262), and Cybersecurity (ISO 21434) standards. Since 2019, our company has been operating in a 500 m² office located in Istanbul Teknopark, which includes a standards-compliant electronics laboratory, HIL test setup, anechoic chamber, and system rooms. With our ISO 9001-certified Quality Management System and ISO 12207/15288 – CMMI v1.3 Level 2 compliant Process Management System, we deliver reliable solutions tailored to our customers’ needs.

about: Connect multiple inputs to a single output, or multiple outputs to a single input, to simplify wiring in automated test systems.PXI Multiplexer Switch Modules are ideal for high-channel-count applications that need to connect measurement or signal generation instruments to various test points on devices or units under test (DUTs or UUTs). PXI Multiplexer Switch Modules use a variety of relay types, including electromechanical armature relays, reed relays,  field-effect transistor (FET) relays, and solid-state relays, each with their own benefits, allowing you to choose a multiplexer that fits your requirements. To program the switches, you can use the IVI-compliant NI-SWITCH driver software, complete with help documentation, example programs, and a soft front panel application for interactive control of switches. For intelligent management of complex switch systems, NI Switch Executive provides additional software tools to help you design, build, and deploy your switching system.

about: HAVELSAN, established in 1982, is one of Türkiye’s leading defence and advanced technology companies. With deep expertise in mission-critical systems, the company continues to deliver cutting-edge technologies that contribute to Türkiye’s technological independence and global competitiveness. HAVELSAN delivers high value-added solutions across a comprehensive portfolio that spans defence technologies, simulation and training systems, autonomous unmanned systems, information and communication technologies, and cybersecurity. As Türkiye’s sole and proven Combat Management Systemprovider, HAVELSAN strengthens national deterrence through advanced platforms like the ADVENT system, while expanding its influence globally through defence exports and international collaborations. With its “AI for Defence” approach, HAVELSAN increasingly integrates artificial intelligence into every layer of its products and services. As a global simulation leader, HAVELSAN offers turnkey training systems including pilot, tank, submarine, and combat simulators that enhance the operational readiness of the Turkish Armed Forces and allied institutions. In the field of autonomy, the company has introduced the “Digital Troops”concept, powered by Hybrid Swarm Intelligence, through land, air, and naval systems such as BARKAN, KAPGAN, BAHA, BOZBEY, and SANCAR AUSV, all designed to respond to next-generation operational challenges. With a presence in nearly 30 countries, HAVELSAN continues to expand its international footprint across Asia-Pacific, the Middle East, Africa, and Latin America. Backed by a strong R&D culture and qualified human capital, the company collaborates with academic institutions, invests in innovation, and commits to shaping the future of secure technologies. Driven by the vision of “Coding the Trusted Future” HAVELSAN remains a trusted solution partner in national and foreign defence and the public and private sectors.

The Hernon slogan is “ Formulating Solutions .” This reflects Hernon’s skill in formulating custom adhesives and sealants but also shows that the Hernon team goes beyond chemical compounds and takes a compressive look at a customer’s needs. name: HUKD

about: Established in 2010 as Turkey’s first aviation cluster, the Aerospace Cluster Association (ACA) carries out a wide range of activities on both national and international scales to support the growth of the aerospace and defence industries. Although headquartered in Izmir, ACA hosts numerous members from various provinces across Turkey, united by two core strategic objectives: "developing a pool of qualified technical talent within the sector" and "enhancing the innovation capabilities of its members—particularly SMEs—to ensure their competitive integration into the global supply chain." The development of Small and Medium-Sized Enterprises (SMEs) and supporting their participation in the aerospace, space, and defence sectors are among the Association’s top strategic priorities. In this context, we contribute to our members’ inclusion in the highly competitive global supply chain by supporting their certification needs (such as AS 9100, NADCAP, etc.), facilitating their access to sectoral supply opportunities and Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs), and assisting them in securing local and international funding. Furthermore, ACA is one of the founding members of the European Aerospace Clusters Partnership (EACP), which currently comprises 45 clusters from 18 countries. Through the EACP network and its global initiative, the "Global Aerospace Clusters Partnership" (GACP), our Cluster participates in international events to consult on shared industry challenges and develops collaborative partnerships through EU-funded and other sponsored projects. In line with this vision, ACA has taken part in numerous projects in collaboration with EACP member clusters, including the "2013 Leonardo da Vinci – Partnerships" project. Another successful initiative conducted with EACP clusters was "HipAir" in 2016, followed by the "SKY 4.0" project, which aimed at developing soft skills to meet the requirements of Industry 4.0. Additionally, we maintain an ongoing cooperation agreement with the Polish aviation cluster, "Aviation Valley." Among the services we offer to our members are international events organized in coordination with stakeholders such as ESBAŞ (Aegean Free Zone). The primary goal of these events is to bring our members together with national and international OEMs. Most recently, at the "Conference on the Development of Our Domestic and National Industry in the Defence and Aerospace Sector through Global Collaborations," held on October 10-11, 2019, with the support of the Presidency of Defence Industries (SSB), we hosted representatives from global giants such as Boeing, Airbus, Rolls-Royce, and GE. Additionally, procurement officials from Turkish Armed Forces Foundation (TSKGV) companies (including TUSAŞ, Havelsan, Roketsan, Aselsan, etc.) attended, resulting in highly successful B2B (Business-to-Business) meetings for our members. As a recent initiative following the COVID-19 pandemic—which emphasized virtual networking—ACA has been organizing online events to promote member activities and strengthen the network between buyers and Turkish suppliers. Furthermore, since 2024, we have been co-organizing "Breakfast Chats" with ESBAŞ to foster more frequent engagement with our members and evaluate global trends. These bimonthly events feature a keynote speaker and two sponsors. With over 80 company representatives in attendance, these gatherings have become a highly preferred platform for our members. As of January 2026, ACA has a total of 67 members, 47 of which are sectoral companies. bu firms primarily operate in the defence, aerospace, space, and automotive industries. In addition to our corporate members, our association includes individual members consisting of esteemed academics from prominent universities, R&D centers, and senior experts from the sectors we serve.

IGU, which advocates that higher education is the most important tool for sustainable development, today offers its national and international students: 7 faculties, 1 college, 2 vocational schools, 85 undergraduate, 68 associate degree regular education programs, 33 associate degree second education programs, 41 thesis-based / 39 non-thesis graduate programs, and 11 doctoral programs under the Graduate Education Institute. Established on February 17, 2011, by Law No. 6114, Istanbul Gelişim University was founded with the goal of becoming a "world university" in the 2026-2027 academic year, with 7 faculties, a Graduate School, 1 college, and 2 vocational schools. With its solid steps toward becoming a globally influential university, Istanbul Gelişim University continues to grow each year and is one of the most preferred universities among Turkey's foundation universities. Achieving the distinction of being Turkey's most accredited university, Istanbul Gelişim University has also attracted worldwide attention with its steps towards internationalization. Located in Istanbul, the financial and commercial center of Turkey and the world, Istanbul Gelişim University offers its students a huge research complex consisting of 150 laboratories. It also has 22 research centers and 80+ student clubs that enable students to engage in social, cultural, scientific, and sporting activities outside of class. Students participating in the International Joint Degree Programs are entitled to double degrees by completing half of their education at partner universities in the USA. Offering double major and minor opportunities at many departments, ITU has the highest number of the Erasmus exchange programs with nearly 1000 partnerships. ITU is home to science-industry-technology with more than 2500 R&D projects carried out in ARI Teknokent. The first cube satellite, the first electric minibus, the first hydrogen-powered boat, the first unmanned automobile, and the first domestic computer were manufactured at ITU. Besides, Turkey’s first television broadcast was aired from ITU, and the first university radio was founded by ITU. ITU’s institutional history, intellectual memory and its esteemed environment builds a strong bridge from the past to the future. ARI TTO: For all the companies and entrepreneurs within İTÜ ARI Teknokent, addressing their needs related to project incentives, collaboration activities, academic consultancy, intellectual property rights, and cluster groups. about: Since our founding in 2014, Ingiant has been dedicated to providing highly reliable rotary connectivity solutions for sophisticated equipment. We are more than a manufacturer; we are an integrated technology partner offering comprehensive R&D, manufacturing, and testing capabilities. Our product line encompasses a full range of products, including servo turntables, simulation turntable systems, measurement and control systems, electrical slip rings, fluid (hydraulic/pneumatic) rotary unions, fiber optic rotary joints (FORJs), and high-frequency RF rotary joints. This extensive portfolio makes us a one-stop customized solution provider for solving various rotary transmission challenges. Supported by a dedicated team of 200 professionals and operating from a modern production facility spanning 30,000 square meters, we possess the complete in-house manufacturing capability—from precision component machining to final system integration. This solid infrastructure guarantees consistent batch delivery and exceptional product quality. Our production and quality management systems strictly adhere to ISO 9001 and international high standards. All products are designed and validated against mainstream international standards such as MIL-STD-810G (environmental engineering) and MIL-STD-461G (EMC), ensuring absolute reliability in the most demanding conditions. Jiujiang Ingiant’s comprehensive strength in developing high-precision inertial navigation and simulation test equipment has reached international advanced levels, and we have achieved mass production capacity for test and simulation turntables. Meanwhile, we have overcome technical challenges in the integrated transmission of optical, electrical, pneumatic, hydraulic and microwave signals under rotary connection conditions, providing customers with highly reliable integrated slip ring solutions. Our solutions are combat-proven, having been successfully deployed in critical platforms including radar antennas, satellite communication vehicles, and integrated vehicles, with a track record of stable batch integration. This is powered by our continuous innovation, driven by an R&D team of 25 engineers, an annual R&D investment exceeding USD 1.2 million, and a portfolio of 58 related patents. We fully understand the specific requirements of the rotary connection industry, strictly comply with international and client-nation trade regulations and confidentiality laws, and are prepared to provide on-site technical assistance to ensure the success of your project.

For more than 30 years in the defence and aerospace industry, KALEKALIP has been the stakeholder of important global projects, contractor of national projects and the incubator center of many important projects. In KALEKALIP, Türkiye’s first national infantry rifle (MPT-76) was designed and produced. Meeting the needs of our military and security forces with MPT76, KALEKALIP continues to take place in the inventory of the state and contributes to nationalization and localization with indigenous products. Completely designed and developed in KALEKALIP taking into account user feedbacks and needs, KALEKALIP Combat Rifle (KCR) series products (KCR556, KCR739, KCR762) have been widely used by Turkish Armed Forces and General Directorate of Security for more than 6 years. KALEKALIP is also supplying 40mm Automatic Grenade Launcher (KAGL40) to Turkish Gendarmerie, Turkish Land Forces and Turkish Naval Forces for more than 20 years. Other in house designed, tested qualified, produced and battle proven product portfolio consists 12,7mm NATO Bolt Action Sniper Rifle (KSR50), 7.62mm NATO Designated Marksman Rifle (KMR762), 40mm Under Barrel Grenade Launcher (KGL40) and 5.56mm and 7.62mm NATO Light Machine Guns (KMG556 & KMG762). Being in the service of Turkish Security Forces for more than 30 years, KALEKALIP is dedicated to enhance the capabilities of Armed Forces, to indigenously develop new light weapons in accordance with field needs/ feedbacks and to present state of the art technologies to the usage of National Forces.

about: Kıraç Galvaniz was established in Bursa in 2006 and operates in the fields of hot-dip galvanizing, steel systems manufacturing, and high-standard metal production solutions for the defense industry. Starting its journey as the first hot-dip galvanizing plant in the region, the company has evolved into a reliable manufacturing partner serving strategic industries, particularly the defense sector, with its strong engineering infrastructure and integrated production capabilities. Since 2010, Kıraç Galvaniz has expanded its product portfolio by entering the steel guardrail manufacturing sector, adding innovative solutions such as motorcycle protection barriers, noise barriers, pedestrian railings, solar mounting systems, and tracker W-Beam profiles. In addition, the company has strengthened its position in the defense sector by manufacturing ground support equipment, antenna and sensor poles, surveillance towers, ammunition boxes, container systems, and precision-machined metal components. With its 4,166 m² galvanizing facility in Bursa and 54,476 m² integrated manufacturing plant in Bozüyük, Bilecik, Kıraç Galvaniz has an annual capacity of 200,000 tons for metal processing and hot-dip galvanizing. Today, the company exports to more than 20 countries and continues to strengthen its global presence through its wholly owned subsidiaries in Georgia and Romania, maintaining its position as a reliable international solution partner. As one of the first companies in Türkiye to obtain CE Certification for guardrail systems, Kıraç Galvaniz successfully completed all required crash tests in compliance with EN 1317 standards, ensuring the certification of its products according to international quality requirements. In 2024, Kıraç Galvaniz successfully completed its public offering on Borsa İstanbul, reinforcing its sustainable growth strategy with strong production infrastructure and continuous R&D investments. Strengthening its competence in the defense sector, Kıraç Galvaniz obtained the EYDEP Certification in 2023, officially demonstrating its capability as a reliable supplier for defense industry projects. Furthermore, the company successfully completed the pre-qualification stage of the supplier registration process conducted by the NATO Support and Procurement Agency (NSPA) and was accepted into the agency’s electronic procurement portal. Kıraç Galvaniz has also been registered in the MKE tender portal, gaining the right to participate in procurement tenders. Within this scope, Kıraç Galvaniz provides solutions for the defense industry in the following areas: Ground Support Equipment Antenna and Sensor Poles Container Systems Surveillance Towers Steel Products for Port and Coastal Infrastructure Metal Injection Parts Electronic Card Design and Component Assembly CNC Machining Ammunition Boxes With its strong engineering infrastructure, integrated manufacturing capabilities, and quality-oriented approach, Kıraç Galvaniz continues its operations with the vision of becoming a reliable domestic manufacturing force in the defense industry.

name: KOSGEB
website: Presidency of the Small and Medium Enterprises Development and Support Administration of the Republic of Turkey Hacı Bayram Mah. İstanbul Cad. No: 32 06050 Ulus / Altındağ / ANKARA
about: KOSGEB from Yesterday to Today The foundations of KOSGEB were laid within the scope of the Small Industry Publication Services Development (KÜSGET) and Industrial Education and Development Center (SEGEM) projects. Established in Gaziantep in 1973 as a pilot project under the Ministry of Industry and Trade within the framework of the International Agreement between the Government of the Republic of Turkey and the United Nations Industrial Development Organization, KÜSGEM began providing services to small-scale industrial enterprises through shared workshops. These efforts were subsequently evaluated, and KÜSGET General Directorate commenced operations in accordance with the International Agreement on the "Development of Small Industry Publication Services," approved by the Council of Ministers Decision No. 83/6744 dated June 17, 1983. KÜSGET provided technical consulting services through Small and Medium-Sized Enterprise Development Centers. KÜSGET implemented modern management principles in these enterprises and carried out services related to strengthening the technical infrastructure for imparting contemporary management skills, working towards establishing and spreading a world-class quality awareness, raising the level of technological production, and increasing employment capacity. SEGEM General Directorate began its activities in accordance with the International Agreement on the Provision of Industrial Education Services, which was approved and entered into force by the Council of Ministers' decision dated November 10, 1978, and numbered 7/16728. SEGEM organized numerous training programs to meet the training needs of SMEs. Based on the importance of the education factor, it provided services to promote lifelong learning among employees and managers of small and medium-sized enterprises. Due to reasons such as the temporary nature of these services, which KÜSGET and SEGEM attempted to provide within the framework of their project agreements, and their inability to sufficiently meet the needs felt throughout the country, Law No. 3624 on the Establishment of the Small and Medium-Sized Industry Development and Support Administration (KOSGEB) was drafted and published in the Official Gazette dated April 20, 1990, and numbered 20498. Established in 1990 by Law No. 3624, KOSGEB provided services and support to SMEs in the manufacturing industry from that date until 2009. However, the increasing potential of sectors other than the manufacturing industry in our country to generate economic added value and create employment, as well as the demands from SMEs in sectors other than the manufacturing industry, brought to the agenda the need to expand KOSGEB's target group to cover all SMEs. In this context, with the enactment of "Law No. 5891 on Amendments to Law No. 3624 on the Establishment of KOSGEB" published in the Official Gazette dated May 5, 2009, and numbered 27219 entered into force, SMEs in the service and trade sectors were also included in KOSGEB's target audience, and KOSGEB became the national institution responsible for SMEs in our country. By 2017, KOSGEB began to focus on supporting businesses operating in the industrial sector, particularly in technological fields and domestic production. Today, it continues to provide the necessary support for SMEs to engage in technology-focused production using domestic and national resources.

about: Shanxi Xinhua Anti-chemical Equipment Research Institute Co., Ltd. (hereinafter referred to as "Anti-chemical Equipment Research Institute") is affiliated to China National Arms Industry Corporation. It is located in Jiantangquan District, Taiyuan City, Shanxi Province. It is one of the 156 key projects built by the former Soviet Union during the "First Five-Year Plan" period; it was established in 1953 and put into operation in 1958. In 2003, it implemented the separation of military and civilian products and changed from the "factory system" to the "company system", and registered as Shanxi Xinhua Chemical Co., Ltd. In 2016, it carried out capital operation and asset reorganization with Beihua Co., Ltd. (a listed company), and officially became a listed company in 2017. In 2020, at the request of China National Arms Industry Corporation, it was officially renamed as Shanxi Xinhua Anti-chemical Equipment Research Institute Co., Ltd. The Anti-chemical Equipment Research Institute covers an area of 1150,000 square meters and has nearly 2,000 employees. It is the only "double guarantee military" unit in China that retains military product research and production capabilities. It is a key enterprise in national anti-chemical equipment research and manufacturing. about: Mensan Defence Aerospace Technology Construction Industry and Trade Inc. is a Turkish technology company focused on developing advanced technological solutions for the defence and aerospace sectors. With its R&D and innovation-driven approach, Mensan Defence Aerospace aims to contribute to the development of indigenous and original technologies in Turkey’s defence industry and to produce competitive products for global markets. Mensan Defence Aerospace has established itself as a trusted solution partner in defence industry projects, with capabilities in system engineering, electronic and mechanical design, software development, prototyping, testing, and system integration. Mensan Defence Aerospace focuses on developing critical systems required by defence platforms, with particular expertise in command and control infrastructures, operator consoles, system integration, and specialized electronic solutions. Main Areas of Expertise •	Engineering solutions for command, control, and communication systems •	Operator consoles and mission station design for defence platforms (underwater, surface, air, and land) •	Electronic system design and manufacturing •	Intelligent power distribution and energy management systems •	Time management systems (NTP analog and digital clocks, NTP server) •	Target and training systems The KONMEN K-202 fanless compact console is an indigenously designed, developed, and patented product, specifically engineered for command and control systems, combat management systems, and situational awareness applications. The fanless cooling architecture enables completely silent and reliable operation in high-temperature environments (up to 50°C). The design reduces the risk of dust and moisture ingress while minimizing maintenance requirements. These features make the system particularly well suited for underwater platforms as well as enclosed, low-noise, and vibration-prone defence environments. The system is designed with an Intelligent Power Distribution Unit, a large multi-display screen, a secondary touchscreen display, service-friendly openable structures for ease of maintenance, a programmable keyboard, and similar features, enabling additional operational capabilities. The MENSAN TIMEN Time Management System includes a TIMEN Digital NTP Clock, a TIMEN Analog NTP Clock, and a TIMEN NTP Server. The NTP Server synchronizes with GNSS satellite systems and distributes precise time information over the network in accordance with the NTP protocol. The NTP clocks display the accurate time data received from the server. The system provides a cost-effective solution for platforms and facilities requiring precise and reliable time synchronization. Mensan Defence Aerospace holds EYDEP Certification, Facility Security Clearance, TEMPEST Certification, and ISO 9001 Quality Management System Certification.

The primary objective of Meteksan Defence is to develop and manufacture indigenous, high-technology products and subsystems to meet the needs of the Turkish Armed Forces and security forces, in line with the vision of the Presidency of Defence Industries. The systems and subsystems developed by the company are used in critical mission areas such as command and control, communications, reconnaissance, surveillance, and intelligence, and are integrated into a wide range of platforms, including surface and underwater platforms, helicopters, and unmanned aerial vehicles. Developing advanced technology products in diverse fields such as radar systems, electronic warfare technologies, communication solutions, training simulators and underwater acoustic systems, Meteksan Defence offers reliable and innovative solutions to its users through its strong engineering infrastructure and high production capability. Meteksan Defence’s product portfolio includes mission-proven systems such as UAV radars, perimeter surveillance radar families, counter-drone systems, tactical data links, anti-jamming solutions, sonar systems, and advanced training simulators. These systems are not only in active service within Türkiye but are also exported to a growing number of friendly and allied countries across different regions, contributing to international cooperation and defence capability development. With more than a decade of experience and the successful completion of numerous national and international projects, Meteksan Defence has established itself as a reliable solution partner in complex and high-technology defence programs. At the core of Meteksan Defence’s success lies its strong engineering capability, highly qualified human resources, and a robust ecosystem built on university–industry collaboration. By maintaining close cooperation with stakeholders, the company continues to deliver reliable, cost-effective, and high-performance systems tailored to operational needs, while ensuring timely execution of projects. As a Bilkent Holding company, Meteksan Defence holds a unique position where all generated resources are transferred to Bilkent University. This structure not only supports the advancement of education by enabling more students to benefit from scholarships and modern educational opportunities, but also reflects the company’s broader commitment to contributing to societal development alongside technological advancement. Today, Meteksan Defence continues its journey with the vision of becoming a globally recognized and trusted defence technology provider. By strengthening international collaborations through joint production and technology transfer models, the company contributes to the development of local defence industries in allied nations while reinforcing its position as a strategic and reliable partner on the global stage. about: In the current market environment, we are increasingly aware that competitors from Asia—particularly manufacturers based in China and Taiwan—are aggressively approaching our customers, resulting in an even more intense competitive landscape. These trends are not limited to Japan but are becoming evident across all regions, including Asia, North America, and Europe. Furthermore, equipment manufacturers that use our products are themselves losing projects to competing equipment manufacturers from China, Taiwan, and other parts of Asia. Under these circumstances, it is no longer sufficient to pursue incremental “improvements” along conventional lines. Instead, we are convinced that we must accelerate and execute fundamental “reforms” that relentlessly address customer needs. With the objective of surviving and thriving amid this intensifying competition, we initiated a comprehensive structural reform program in fiscal year 2022. As of August 2026, this initiative will enter its fifth year. At present, we have positioned “Maximizing Price × Quality × Lead Time × Communication Capability” as our core theme and are working to further elevate the level of our structural reforms. In the past, we held an overly optimistic assumption that if our quality remained stable, customers would choose us even when our price and lead time were not competitive. However, in today’s fiercely competitive market, maintaining a high level of quality is merely a given. We have therefore renewed our recognition that it is essential to refine and elevate all four elements—Price, Quality, Lead Time, and Communication Capability—to a consistently high level. While maintaining high quality, we are challenging ourselves to enhance productivity from every possible perspective, thereby upgrading our competitiveness in both price and lead time. In addition to improving the lead time of finished products, we will also raise our performance to meet customer expectations in terms of lead times for drawing preparation, quotations, and delivery-date responses. Moreover, rather than simply promoting product performance, we are striving to strengthen our communication capability with customers through proposals focused on product compactness, labor reduction, overall cost optimization, and customized solutions. By maximizing Price × Quality × Lead Time × Communication Capability, we will clearly differentiate ourselves from highly agile Asian manufacturers. As a result, we are confident that the presence and value of “Made in Japan” quality will be further enhanced. We are continuously exposed to various uncertainties, including domestic political instability, additional tariffs in the United States, and a global economic slowdown. However, rather than waiting for economic recovery or political stability, which are beyond our control, we will place self-transformation at the center of our agenda. By igniting the second engine of our structural reforms, we will proactively and decisively move forward.

about: 4U Pro Gen 5 – GPU acceleratorThe 4U Pro combines the power of the latest PCIe Gen 5 add-in cards with an optimized, feature-rich, and semi-rugged design for the most demanding HPC edge applications. The appliance supports up to 8 NVIDIA H100 PCIe GPUs which deliver 2.5x FP64 performance compared to the NVIDIA H100, and four PCIe Gen 5 x16 HIB/NIC slots for up to 256GB/s of sustained data throughput. Alternatively, the 4U Pro can be configured to provide 16 single-width PCIe Gen 5 x8 slots for FPGA data ingest or the latest storage add-in cards. The Gen 5 4UP also includes the OSS developed U-BMC with value-add features including dynamic fan speed control and IPMI based system monitoring which integrates seamlessly with the host server system management. Additional features include solid and vented filler panel options (purchasable in quantities of 17 or 18) and replaceable fan filters. The chassis design is ‘semi-rugged’ suitable to use outside a typical datacenter environment. The Gen 5 4UP is the ideal expansion platform for the entire AI workflow at the edge.

about: Located in in Izmir Gaziemir Free Zone in Turkey and established in 2000, Oz Optics A.S. is a part of OZ Optics Limited which is a leading worldwide supplier of fiber optic products for existing and next-generation optical networks. In addition to designing and manufacturing components and test equipment for fiber optics markets, the company offers award-winning fiber optic sensor systems for remote monitoring of oil and gas pipelines, wells, refineries, bridges, dams and other large structures, security fences and for fire detection. OZ Optics manufactures its products in three locations, Ottawa (Canada), Izmir (Turkey), and Jiaxing (China). Our seven different product groups are marketed and sold globally to over 10,000 customers in over 60 countries, through our well-established and extensive network of sales representatives and distributors, as well as through direct customer engagement. Our fiber optic product lines include: Components for OE Packaging, Attenuators, Laser-to-Fiber Delivery Components, Polarization Maintaining Components, Test Equipment, Fiber Optic Sensors, High Power Components, and Fiber Optic Components for OCT Applications, Gyroscopes, Quantum, BioPhotonics & AI Optical Connectivity. By leveraging the technology and expertise gained since its inception, OZ Optics has attracted a broad range of customers in the telecommunications, cable television, medical, military, security, industrial, construction, aerospace, power utilities, petrochemical and educational sectors. Our products are based on proprietary technology, trade secrets and patents. OZ Optics was the first company to introduce polarization maintaining fiber optic components, test equipment and visual fault locators to the marketplace, and it was also the first company to integrate wireless technology into its designs of remote fiber optic measurement systems and instruments. Over its 41-year history, its peers in the industry and in the community have recognized OZ Optics with numerous awards for innovation and achievement, including most recently the 2009 Frost & Sullivan Best Practices Award for our Distributed Strain and Temperature Sensor. OZ Optics is ISO 9001:2015 certified. OZ Optics continues its leadership role in polarization maintaining components, fiber optic sensors and test equipment. The company is also well known for its custom design work.

name: ÖZYEĞİN ÜNİVERSİTESİ
industry: Universities and Research Institutions
address: Çekmeköy Kampüsü Nişantepe Mah. Orman Sk. 34794 Çekmeköy - İstanbul
about: Founded in 2007 in Istanbul, Türkiye, as a non-profit foundation university, Özyeğin University (ÖzÜ) is strategically positioned with the objective of becoming one of the most innovative and research-intensive universities in Türkiye. Within the Entrepreneurial and Innovative University Index, prepared under the leadership of the Scientific and Technological Research Council of Türkiye (TÜBİTAK), ÖzÜ ranked 6th in 2024 and has consistently maintained a position among the top ten universities since the inception of the index. According to the 2024 Times Higher Education Impact Rankings, ÖzÜ is placed among the top 400 universities worldwide and is recognized as one of the top five universities in Türkiye in terms of its contribution to global sustainable development goals. ÖzÜ comprises seven faculties—the Faculty of Engineering, Faculty of Social Sciences, Faculty of Law, Faculty of Business, Faculty of Aviation and Aeronautical Sciences, Faculty of Architecture and Design, and Faculty of Applied Sciences—alongside three graduate institutes (the Graduate School of Engineering, the Graduate School of Business, and the Graduate School of Social Sciences), one academic unit dedicated to language education (the School of Foreign Languages), and eight research centers. These research centers include the Center for Computational Finance; the Center for Energy, Environment and Economy (CE³); the Energy Efficient LED Lighting Research, Development, Education and Application Center (EVATEG); the Optical Wireless Communication Technologies Applied Research Center of Excellence (OKATEM); the Technology Products Center; the Entrepreneurship Center; the Alp Alkaş Retail and Real Estate Center; and the German Law Application and Research Center. The university community currently consists of approximately 7,700 undergraduate students, 523 master’s students, and 205 doctoral candidates, supported by 521 academic staff members and 375 administrative personnel. In 2012, the Technology Transfer Office (TTO) was established to strengthen ÖzÜ’s capacity as a research-driven university and to facilitate the translation of academic knowledge into societal and economic value. The TTO provides comprehensive support to researchers in preparing national and international grant applications, fosters university–industry collaboration, and manages the university’s intellectual and industrial property rights (IPR) portfolio. In addition, ÖzÜ launched its first startup acceleration program, Start-up Factory, aimed at supporting the development, mentoring, and scaling of high-potential and sustainable entrepreneurial ventures. Complementary units such as Openfab, the Continuing Education Center, the Sustainability Platform, and the Gender Equality Office provide advisory services and operational support to academics, researchers, and students, while actively contributing to the implementation of interdisciplinary projects. As of March 2026, ÖzÜ’s research portfolio comprises 1,135 projects with a total budget of 490.19 million TRY. The majority of this funding is secured through European Union programs and TÜBİTAK funding schemes, while the remaining portion originates from industry partnerships and other national and international funding bodies. Since 2013, ÖzÜ has filed 44 patent and utility model applications at both national and international levels, 21 of which were submitted jointly with industry partners and other universities. The university currently maintains an intellectual property portfolio consisting of 199 granted patents and 8 registered utility models. Furthermore, since 2020, 14 patents from this portfolio have been successfully commercialized through technology transfer mechanisms.

name: PEARSON ELECTRONICS
address: Pearson Electronics | 4009 Transport Street, Palo Alto, CA 94303
about: Pearson Electronics is the original and leading manufacturer of precision current-monitoring transformers used for accurate AC current measurements. The company was founded in 1955 by Dr. Paul Pearson, inventor and patent holder, of the precision wide-band current transformer.  The Pearson design plus careful workmanship and quality control produce current monitors with excellent frequency response and amplitude accuracy. Originally developed for measuring pulse-currents, Pearson Current Monitors™ are now also widely used to measure more complicated transients and periodic signals from a few Hertz to well into the megaHertz region. Pearson Electronics has long been considered the market leader in the design and manufacturing of Wide-Band Current Transformers used for accurate AC current measurements. Pearson Current Transformers can measure transients, harmonics, pulse, sine-wave, RF and other complex current wave shapes. A frequency range from 0.7 Hz to 350 MHz is covered, with our most popular model covering 1 Hz to 20 MHz ± 3 dB. 1% accuracy, or better, is obtainable over the mid-band. The current range is from micro-amps to kilo-amps and we manufacture both toroid and clamp-on Current Transformers. We stock a large variety of models for immediate delivery and design and manufacture for both OEM and custom requirements. Pearson Electronics also specializes in the design of High Voltage Pulse Transformers that are used with high power microwave tubes and Capacitive Voltage Dividers. These transformers employ open construction and are intended to be used in high voltage insulating oil. Pulse output voltages range from 100kV to 500kV with pulse lengths from 0.25 to 50 microseconds. Pearson Electronics is proud to have a Quality Management System that is accredited to ISO 9001:2015.  This accreditation was completed by NQA, a leading global independently accredited certification body providing assessments of organizations to various Management System Standards since 1988.

Since its establishment, KOLT has positioned itself as a strategic solutions partner, strengthening Türkiye’s defence ecosystem through national engineering capabilities and platform-oriented solutions. KOLT designs and manufactures stable, reliable, and military-standard-compliant power solutions required by systems operating on land, naval, and airborne platforms. These solutions are developed to deliver high performance even under harsh environmental conditions and intense operational loads. KOLT’s core areas of expertise include military-standard-compliant AC/DC and DC/DC power supplies, DC/DC converters, battery charging units and power units, as well as adaptable high-power solutions for varying power requirements. All products are designed and developed in-house by our experienced R&D engineers. This approach enables fast configuration and customization to meet customer requirements. High efficiency, compact design, effective thermal management, EMI/EMC compliance, and durability under harsh field conditions are among the key criteria in our product development processes. Our systems are designed and validated in accordance with relevant military environmental and electromagnetic compatibility standards, particularly MIL-STD-810, MIL-STD-461, MIL-STD-1275, and MIL-STD-704. Thanks to our manufacturing infrastructure in Türkiye, prototyping, testing, and mass production processes are carried out in a controlled, traceable, and quality-driven manner. This domestic production capability contributes to reducing foreign dependency in critical subsystems while also supporting supply security in defence projects.

After our presentation at the Future Forces trade fair in Prague in October 2022, the Rescue Trailer attracted a lot of attention and interest not only from Czech and Slovak delegations, for whom we are currently preparing a logistics demonstration day, but also from representatives of armies and rescue services from all over the world. It is an innovative solution of a storable, robust and affordable trailer that can be equipped according to the customer's specifications and adapted to their needs. about: RobotEye was founded in 2023 at ODTÜ Teknokent with the aim of developing new and disruptive technologies domestically. The company’s mission is to transform conventional approaches in the fields of surveillance, monitoring, and analysis. By developing state-of-the-art software, electronic, and robotic systems using proprietary AI technologies, the company aims to enhance defence capabilities, ensure public safety, and protect the environment. Dual-use functionality lies at the core of the company’s innovation. The products developed not only protect societies but also serve the purpose of protecting our planet. With in-house product design, rapid prototyping, and production capabilities, RobotEye aims to become a global brand in its field with its experienced and expert staff. Using domestically developed software and hardware, the company offers artificial intelligence-powered video/image analysis, real-time intelligence, situational awareness, and decision-support capabilities. These systems are utilized for purposes such as international, border, and domestic security, migrant control, critical facility security, and the protection of wildlife. In areas with no or limited communication capabilities, objects such as people, animals, and vehicles are detected using edge AI and transmitted to users via satellite communication. Additionally, commercially available WiFi and GSM-based security cameras and trail cameras can be integrated into the system. By combining all data with central analysis and decision-support software, a real-time situational awareness map covering the entire globe is generated and presented to decision-makers.

about: About the Sabancı University Nanotechnology Research and Application Center (SUNUM) The Sabancı University Nanotechnology Research and Application Center (SUNUM) is a National Research Infrastructure established in 2010 by the Ministry of Development of the Republic of Turkey and the Sabancı Foundation, and has been operating under Law No. 6550 since 2017. SUNUM possesses an infrastructure capable of serving various sectors across different disciplines through its expertise in the design, synthesis, and characterization of nanomaterials and nanostructures, as well as the design and production of micro-nano systems developed from nanostructures. SUNUM was granted its second five-year accreditation in December 2022. SUNUM Laboratories, which house a wide range of advanced equipment featuring rare technologies both in Turkey and worldwide, offer research opportunities and services in various fields, including atomic-resolution imaging, rapid gene sequencing for different cell types, the multilayer deposition of metals just a few atoms thick, and dielectric applications. As of 2023, SUNUM collaborates with nearly 400 institutions—approximately 300 domestic and 100 international—and conducts research and development activities across a wide range of application areas, spanning from medicine to chemistry, pharmaceuticals to energy, cosmetics to automotive, energy to agriculture, and food to the environment. SUNUM serves as the lead organization in two different TÜBİTAK 1004 Platforms (NANOSIS—Integrated, Scalable, Functional Nanostructures and Systems; LignoNANO—Value-Added Advanced Nanotechnological Materials and Systems for a Sustainable Circular Economy) and participates as a partner in three other 1004 Platforms. Additionally, it serves as the coordinator for the NANOBIO4CAN Project—Nano-Biotechnologies for Innovative Therapeutic Approaches in Cancer—under the Marie Sklodowska-Curie – COFUND program.Coordinated by SUNUM, the project titled “Teaming for Capacity Development and Synergies in Micro-Nanofabrication and Flexible Electronics for Widespread Impact—TeamNANO,” funded under the Horizon Europe Program’s Teaming call, holds the distinction of being the project with the highest budget ever awarded to a single institution in our country’s history following an evaluation by the European Commission. Scheduled to run for six years, TEAM-NANO aims to enhance SUNUM’s cleanroom equipment infrastructure and technical capabilities through international collaboration. The project is being carried out with a 9 million Euro EU budget and co-financing from the Ministry of Industry and Technology of the Republic of Turkey. SUNUM conducts efficient and effective multidisciplinary and interdisciplinary research and development to create products and services in the field of nanotechnology that have universal applicability and socio-economic value. It fosters the development of qualified human resources, knowledge, technology, intellectual property, and new infrastructure—whether standalone or collaborative—and, when necessary, establishes entrepreneurial companies to facilitate the dissemination of these innovations. Through long-term collaborations that highlight the core competencies of partner industries and academic research institutions and contribute to their development, SUNUM is working to become an open, continuously evolving, sustainable, and globally exemplary and leading Center of Excellence for all stakeholders.

about: With science, technology, and social structures advancing day by day, the world needs more qualified individuals who possess the knowledge and skills required to pursue a career anywhere in the world. Aware of this pressing issue, in 1994 the Sabancı Group decided to establish a "world university" led by the Sabancı Foundation. The seeds of Sabancı University, the Sabancı Group's most comprehensive social responsibility project in the field of education, were sown in the summer of 1995, at a search conference in which 50 academics from 22 countries as well as students and representatives from the private sector participated. The conference, however, was just the beginning, as it marked the start of an extensive process during which the leading educational institutions in Turkey and across the world were examined to find out how they work and identify the fundamentals that made them successful. In the end, instead of choosing one university as a template or replicating existing examples and institutions, a new and unique university was designed. Since 1999, when students began enrolling, Sabancı University itself has set an example for many universities.

about: SAHA MİHENK is the National Aviation Industry Cluster established under SAHA Istanbul with the support of the Ministry of Industry and Technology of the Republic of Türkiye. The cluster aims to strengthen Türkiye’s indigenous and national production capabilities in the defence and aerospace sectors, providing companies with a strategic development platform across quality standards, certification infrastructure, supply chain integration, and global competitiveness. The primary objective of SAHA MİHENK is to enhance the integration of industry stakeholders into both national and international aerospace ecosystems, accelerate quality-driven transformation in production and design processes, and support companies in accessing global markets. In this context, the cluster develops multi-stakeholder collaboration models across manufacturing, design, R&D, quality management, certification, export development, and industrialization. In line with SAHA Istanbul’s role as the Regional Authority of the International Aerospace Quality Group in Türkiye, SAHA MİHENK contributes to companies’ compliance with international quality standards, particularly AS9100, while promoting a sustainable, traceable, and competitive production culture across the sector. Bringing together public institutions, prime contractors, universities, and industrial organizations under a unified development framework, SAHA MİHENK aims to enhance institutional capabilities within the aerospace industry, strengthen certification capacity, and deepen high value-added production infrastructure in line with Türkiye’s National Technology Initiative.

The factory, located in the Düzce Organized Industrial Zone, operates on 120,000 m² with 700 employees in a three-shift production system and utilizes Industry 4.0–compliant processes. Sarsılmaz develops and produces a wide range of systems, from pistols and machine guns to medium-caliber cannon systems and unmanned robotic platforms. With advanced R&D capabilities, the company provides solutions across multiple calibers ranging from 7.65 mm to 30 mm, positioning itself not only as a manufacturer but also as a comprehensive defence solutions provider. Today, Sarsılmaz exports to more than 80 countries, and its products are actively used by military and law enforcement agencies worldwide. Through its group companies — Best Defence, TR Mekatronik, Ünimetal, BPS, and LA2 Dynamics — Sarsılmaz offers integrated capabilities including remote weapon systems, cannon systems, precision manufacturing, ammunition production, and robotic weapon platforms. name: SHGM
website: Eti Mahallesi Gazi Mustafa Kemal Bulvarı No:128/A 06570 Maltepe / ANKARA
about: The first aviation activities in our country began in 1912 in Sefaköy, near today's Atatürk Airport, with two hangars and a small airfield. Adopting M. Kemal Atatürk's words, "THE FUTURE IS IN THE SKIES," which also guided the country's future, the Turkish Aviation Society was founded in 1925 and later renamed the Turkish Aeronautical Association, laying the institutional foundations for Turkish Civil Aviation. The first civil air transport was launched in 1933 with a small fleet of five aircraft under the name "Turkish Air Mail." In the 10th year of our Republic, the "State Airline Administration," established under the Ministry of National Defence, was tasked with establishing civil air routes and providing transportation in Turkey. In response to the rapid development of global civil aviation and major technological advances, the "Civil Aviation Department," established within the Ministry of Transportation in 1954 to protect national interests and ensure the orderly conduct and supervision of international relations, was reorganized in 1987 as the "General Directorate of Civil Aviation" in line with the conditions of the day. Until November 18, 2005, the General Directorate of Civil Aviation was the main service unit of the Ministry of Transportation. On that date, Law No. 5431 on the Organization and Duties of the General Directorate of Civil Aviation came into force, granting it financial autonomy and establishing its current management structure. Today, aviation activities in our country are carried out within the framework of the Turkish Civil Aviation Law No. 2920 and the Administrative and Technical Regulations and Aviation Instructions published within this scope. In the aviation industry , which requires advanced technology and has no boundaries, our country has become a member of various international organizations in order to closely follow international aviation developments and meet the requirements of the era. Our country became a party to the "Chicago Convention on International Civil Aviation," which forms the basis of international civil aviation, in 1945 and is among the founding members of the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO). Furthermore, in the European region, our country, which was a founding member of the European Civil Aviation Conference (ECAC) in 1956, is also a member of the European Organization for the Safety of Air Navigation (EUROCONTROL). Additionally, our country, which is a member of various organizations at the regional level, resolutely continues its aviation activities in accordance with national and international legislation.
