about: SİSTEM Aluminum is a fully integrated aluminum manufacturer operating on a 330,000 m² land area with more than 1,200 employees, specializing in aluminum billet casting, extrusion, architectural façade systems, and aluminum forging. Founded in 1994, SİSTEM exports aluminum solutions to more than 70 countries across four continents. The company’s forging division plays an important role in supplying high-strength aluminum components for the defence, firearms systems, aerospace, automotive, and general industry sectors. Using hot forging technology, SİSTEM Aluminum produces machined or unmachined forged semi-finished parts (forging blanks) for military and civilian platforms. Within this scope, forged parts for air vehicles such as aircraft, helicopters, and UAVs, as well as upper and lower receivers, pistol frames, and feed heads for firearms systems, are produced. In addition, components such as buffer tubes, handguards, and charging handles can be manufactured from extruded profiles either as machined parts or semi-finished products. With multiple forging presses with capacities up to 4,000 tons, SİSTEM Aluminum is capable of producing forgings from 2XXX, 5XXX, 6XXX, and 7XXX series aluminum alloys. The fully integrated production facility includes tool and die design and manufacturing, CAD/CAM engineering, CNC machining, sandblasting, painting, anodizing, and hard anodizing as advanced surface finishing processes. Operating under EN 9100 and IATF 16949 quality systems, SİSTEM is a reliable and long-term supplier to global defence, aerospace, automotive, and industrial OEM, Tier-1, and Tier-2 manufacturers.

about: SpaceForest, based in Gdynia, Poland, specializes in advanced aerospace and microwave technologies. Founded in 2004, the company combines expertise in radar systems, electronics and rocket technologies. In the field of radar and microwave systems, SpaceForest develops high-performance solutions for demanding applications. Its flagship product, the HUSSAR X-band synthetic aperture radar (SAR), is recognized for its precision and reliability in airborne missions. The company also provides comprehensive electronic manufacturing services, from design and prototyping to full-scale production. In addition, SpaceForest is a leader in microwave filter tuning solutions, using proprietary AI-powered software to optimize filter performance for telecommunications systems, significantly improving efficiency compared to traditional manual methods. In rocket technologies, SpaceForest is the developer of the Perun suborbital rocket, capable of carrying payloads of up to 50 kg to an altitude of 150 km. Designed for scientific and commercial missions requiring microgravity conditions, Perun is powered by an eco-friendly hybrid engine, offering both cost-effectiveness and environmental sustainability. The company also develops advanced composite structures engineered to withstand extreme conditions encountered during launch and flight. SpaceForest collaborates with leading institutions, including the European Space Agency (ESA), and holds ISO 9001 and ISO 14001 certifications, ensuring high standards of quality and environmental management.

about: The Industrial Competence Assessment and Support Program (EYDEP) was initiated in 2017 by the Secretariat of Defence Industries with the objective of assessing the industrial competency inventories of companies operating in, or aspiring to enter, the defence industry. Based on these assessments, the program aims to support the development of such companies by providing mentorship and targeted assistance in areas identified as requiring improvement. Within this framework, EYDEP serves the following key functions: • It constitutes a formal entry mechanism for companies seeking to participate in the defence industry. • It provides a comprehensive evaluation system to determine the readiness levels of companies for engagement in the sector. • It acts as a catalyst for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) to enhance their capabilities, achieve competitiveness in international markets, and ensure long-term sustainability. • It establishes a common framework that facilitates effective communication and alignment between prime contractors and subcontractors. • It offers a standardized and sector-wide accepted evaluation methodology. • It functions as a structured development platform that supports the formulation of strategic roadmaps, enabling companies to evolve into integrators within the defence ecosystem.

about: Truflo Marine is a leading designer and manufacturer of high-integrity naval valves and actuators for mission-critical applications on submarines and surface vessels. With more than 60 years of engineering expertise, our solutions help naval operators achieve the highest levels of availability, reliability and maintainability required for continuous fleet readiness for over 37 navies worldwide. We specialise in high performance, application tailored fluid control solutions that meet the demanding requirements of modern naval environments. Our valves deliver precise control, high flow capability and rapid shutoff, supporting essential systems like ballast, cooling, propulsion, weapons handlings systems, auxiliary and many more safety critical systems. Every product is rigorously qualified to stringent quality and environmental standards, ensuring long-term durability, resistance to shock and vibration, and dependable operation throughout the platform lifecycle. Our innovation portfolio includes Acoustic Control Vantage™ (ACV) – acoustic flow control technology, leveraging advanced manufacturing, designed to reduce noise and enhances stealth, while improving reliability and performance in mission critical environments. ACV significantly reduces fluid borne noise and structural vibration, delivering quieter, more effective platforms. Through additive manufacturing techniques that mitigate cavitation, ACV also extends component life and reduces wear, directly contributing to improved availability, reduced maintenance demands and lower through life cost of ownership. Truflo Marine is committed to delivering high-performance technologies that enhance platform readiness and resilience in the world’s most challenging maritime environments.

With more than six decades of experience, TÜBİTAK plays a pivotal role in fostering robust collaboration among industry, academia, and the public sector, while actively promoting the generation of knowledge and advanced technologies through both national and international programs. Through its strategic R&D activities, TÜBİTAK is one of the key actors driving Türkiye’s sustainable development and technological advancement. Defence industry, aviation, and space are among TÜBİTAK’s major areas of strength. Through the development of critical and high-impact technologies in these fields, TÜBİTAK significantly contributes to strengthening Türkiye’s global competitiveness and achieving internationally recognized milestones. Beyond advancing today’s technologies, TÜBİTAK also makes strategic investments in the human capital that will carry these technologies into the future. In alignment with the National Technology Initiative Vision and the objectives of the 12th Development Plan, as well as in pursuit of the 2030 Industry Strategy goals, TÜBİTAK actively promotes a culture of science and innovation across all segments of society. Through flagship platforms such as DENEYAP Technology Workshops, National Technology Workshops, science centers, and TEKNOFEST—the world’s largest aviation, space, and technology festival—TÜBİTAK ensures widespread public engagement with science and technology.

This effort is carried out in line with its mission to provide the Turkish Armed Forces and the national defence industry with high value-added, globally competitive, and nationally developed technologies, products, and services through research and development. Aligned with the mission of “National R&D for National Defence” TÜBİTAK SAGE minimizes reliance on foreign defence technologies and provides the national defence ecosystem with advanced, high-value, cuttingedge systems. With expertise in guidance kits, smart munitions, missile systems, test and measurement laboratories, energetic materials, critical subsystems, and advanced defence technologies, TÜBİTAK SAGE strengthens Türkiye’s defence capability through original design, prototyping, testing, and production activities as the country’s leading defence R&D institution. Driven by the vision of achieving full independence for Türkiye in defence technologies and attaining global competitiveness through innovative solutions, TÜBİTAK SAGE brings together its scientific knowledge, engineering excellence, and the objectives of the National Technology Initiative. In doing so, it continues to lead Türkiye’s journey toward defence self-sufficiency and to pioneer breakthroughs across the national defence industry.

about: TÜBİTAK National Metrology Institute (UME) is a research and development institute operating under the umbrella of the Scientific and Technological Research Council of Türkiye (TÜBİTAK). The aim of the Institute is to ensure equality in national and international trade and to increase the quality of Turkish industrial products and to contribute to the scientific and technological development of Turkey by creating the National Metrology System necessary to secure all measurements made in Turkey, to ensure the integration of these measurements into the international system, to develop existing and new measurement technologies, thus to ensure the accuracy of measurements used in the commercial and industrial environment. For this purpose, the Institute develops and maintains national measurement reference standards, ensures their traceability to the international measurement system, and makes them available to the industry, science and research system. The Institute, which constitutes a reference for measurements made in Turkey and is the highest authority on this subject, cooperates with other units of TÜBİTAK, domestic and foreign organizations in its studies, and takes into consideration the principles of training both its own researchers and technical personnel and the technical personnel of external organizations active in measurement, testing, calibration, analysis, control and metrology.

about: The product portfolio of TÜBİTAK UZAY includes BİLSAT, RASAT, GÖKTÜRK-2 and İMECE satellites that have successfully served our country in the field of earth observation since 2001. Two operational Earth observation satellites, GÖKTÜRK-2 and İMECE consist of subsystems and components, whose design, production and integration processes were carried out in-house. The most important proof of the reliability of these subsystems and the relevant AIT processes (production, integration and testing) used in their development is the RASAT satellite, which has recently completed its service life and was retired. The RASAT satellite, which had a design life of three years, was successfully operated for an impressive 11 years period. Our latest significant success, TÜRKSAT 6A, the first indigenously-developed national telecommunications satellite of Türkiye, was launched to space and placed into geostationary Earth orbit (GEO) in July 2024. Following the successful completion of all in-orbit tests, TÜRKSAT 6A was commissioned in April 2025 and entered service. In addition to developing five satellites that have successfully operated in space (both LEO and GEO), TÜBİTAK UZAY is also at the epicenter of productive cooperation and collaboration at both national and international level in areas such as technology transfer, AIT services and consultancy.

about: The TXT Aerospace & Defence Cluster is part of TXT, an international IT Group and end-to-end provider of consultancy, software services, and solutions that support the digital transformation of customers’ products and core processes. With a proprietary software portfolio and strong expertise in vertical domains, TXT has a growing presence across Aerospace, Aviation, Defence, Industrial and Government markets. Within this ecosystem, the TXT Aerospace & Defence Cluster combines advanced capabilities in design, operations, and training. TXT is a recognised leader in System and Software Engineering for real-time and safety-critical systems. The Cluster also includes a range of Smart Solutions such as VAPS, a recognised leader in Embedded Graphics and HMI; DESMO, a premier platform for aircraft and on-board systems preliminary design; and ACE, which supports aircraft options selection and cabin configuration. In operations, innovative platforms such as FPO optimize flight efficiency, reduce CO₂ emissions, improve performance analysis, and strengthen decision-making. ICARUS is a modular, cloud-based digital twin suite designed for next-generation air mobility systems, bringing together aircraft and drone design, fleet planning, real-time simulation, and mission analysis in a unified environment. Mission focuses on route and aircraft performance analysis and is the only commercially available tool that combines aircraft definition, performance, and cost investigations in a single environment. These capabilities are supported by decades of expertise in Ground Station operations, connectivity, cybersecurity, and DevSecOps. In training, TXT leverages AI and XR technologies through solutions such as WEAVR, for XR training and InstructIQ an intuitive data-driven training analytics tool, alongside a broad range of simulators and trainers for civil and military aviation. Backed by deep engineering know-how, the Cluster supports leading aerospace and defence organisations in their digital transformation journeys, exploring advanced concepts such as the metaverse and digital twin. Headquartered in Milan, TXT has subsidiaries across Europe, Asia and the United States, and its holding company, TXT e-Solutions S.p.A., has been listed on the Italian Stock Exchange, STAR segment (TXT.MI), since July 2000.

Türkiye’s national and local product, the ULAK Base Station, was developed under the main contractorship of ASELSAN in collaboration with NETAŞ and ARGELA. about: ÜTAKSİS is a domestically developed Production Tracking System designed for high-security manufacturing environments, delivering end-to-end traceability across the entire production lifecycle. In the defence, aerospace, and space industries, quality, control, and full recordability are not optional — they are mandatory. ÜTAKSİS is engineered to transform these requirements into operational discipline. From raw materials to final products, all processes are systematically recorded, including: Materials and sub-components used Personnel operations Equipment utilization Production stages and workflow steps As a result: Critical situations can be managed with rapid and controlled backward traceability Revision and version histories are securely maintained Production stages remain bound to authorization and workflow discipline Audit processes become transparent and sustainable Designed in alignment with defence industry requirements, ÜTAKSİS provides: Closed-loop, offline operational capability independent of internet connectivity Role-based authorization and controlled access architecture A process structure that enforces manufacturing discipline ÜTAKSİS transforms production from person-dependent execution into a structured, auditable, and secure system. It is developed to support sustainable quality, secure data governance, and operational control in strategic industries.

about: Forum Energy Technologies (FET) are a world leading designer and manufacturer of subsea systems and have been reliably supporting military organisations and naval forces for more than 45 years. Encompassing two of the leading brands in the design and build of remote intervention vehicles - Perry Slingsby Systems and Sub-Atlantic, FET have supplied over 900 manned and unmanned underwater vehicles. We pioneered the development of manned submersibles since 1975. Previously delivered systems include NSRS (NATO Submarine Rescue System) which entered service in 2008 and “remains the benchmark” according to many of her team. The latest SRV system (LR11) was delivered in 2021 for the Vietnamese Navy and was built to the latest Lloyds design codes to facilitate full class certification. LR11 has increased power and an advanced auxiliary thruster control system that allows for operation in high currents. The system was supplied with a Launch & Recovery System and a complete training & aftersales support package as well as an ROV System, Simulator, ELSS Pod Skid & Mating Target. FET have a long history of supplying ROV’s to the global military market including Intervention ROV’s which are designed to be deployed rapidly to minimize the time taken until first Intervention.
